item.title: Eric Jang – Building AlphaGo from scratch 摘要
item.description:
Eric Jang explains how to build AlphaGo from scratch using modern AI tools, highlighting its role as a clean example of intelligence primitives: search, learning from experience, and self-play. He contrasts AlphaGo's Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) with naive policy gradient RL in LLMs, noting MCTS sidesteps the credit assignment problem by providing better actions at each move. The discussion also covers which parts of AI research LLMs can automate (experiments, hyperparameter tuning) and which they struggle with (choosing research questions, escaping dead ends). The conversation includes insights on RL inefficiency and automated AI researchers.
标签: AlphaGo, reinforcement learning, Monte Carlo Tree Search, LLM, AI research, self-play, credit assignment, Go, deep learning, automation

item.title: Premium: What If...We're In An AI Bubble? (Part 1) 摘要
item.description:
The article criticizes hyperbolic AI predictions, arguing that claims about AGI creating a 'permanent underclass' or eliminating software jobs are unfounded. It debunks myths about Tesla's Optimus robot, METR's time horizon studies, and unrealistic revenue projections for OpenAI and Anthropic. The author highlights a lack of data centers coming online, circular AI revenue among hyperscalers, and the precarious financial positions of companies like Oracle and CoreWeave tied to OpenAI. The piece questions the sustainability of the AI bubble, suggesting massive capital misallocation and potential collapse if promised infrastructure and revenue fail to materialize.
标签: AI bubble, AGI hype, data centers, OpenAI, Anthropic, NVIDIA, hyperscalers, tech criticism, economic analysis, Tesla Optimus

item.title: Aluminium OS: Google’s ‘Android for PC’ OS for Googlebooks 摘要
item.description:
John Gruber discusses Google's Aluminium OS (codename ALOS), a new Android-based OS for laptops and desktops, built from Android 17 with a custom window manager, taskbar, virtual desktops, and integrated Gemini AI. He notes that the aluminium-os.com site is unofficial, but the concept is intriguing. Google confirmed Aluminium is only a codename, not the final branding. Gruber praises the OS's ambition to unify ChromeOS and Android, acknowledging Apple's Continuity success, and highlights features like on-device code assistance and natural language automation, positioning it as a potential developer workstation.
标签: Aluminium OS, Google, Android, ChromeOS, operating system, laptops, desktops, Gemini AI, developer tools, Apple Continuity

item.title: Dropover, a Mac Shelf Utility That Makes Clever Use of Mouse Shaking 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses Dropover, a Mac utility that provides a temporary shelf for dragging and dropping files. It highlights the app's clever use of a mouse-shaking gesture to summon the shelf mid-drag, which the author finds intuitive and convenient. Dropover also offers a modifier key option (Shift by default). The author compares it to Yoink, a similar app, noting that Dropover's shake gesture feels natural and doesn't require using the other hand. Dropover is free with a $7 one-time purchase for full features, while Yoink costs $9. Both are recommended for improving drag-and-drop workflows.
标签: Dropover, Yoink, Mac utility, drag and drop, mouse gesture, productivity, Mac app, shelf, file management, review

item.title: Wanton Destruction of CBS Property 摘要
item.description:
The article is a brief post on Daring Fireball, consisting solely of the quote 'Good night and good luck, motherfuckers.' It links to a story about wanton destruction of CBS property, likely referencing a controversial or defiant statement made in a media context. The post is short and serves as a commentary or reaction to an event involving CBS.
标签: media, commentary, CBS, controversy, quote, news, culture, short-form
